brief: GAP/RATIONALE: Vascular access-site complications are the top cause of delayed discharge after pulmonary vein isolation (PVI); same-day discharge limited by femoral venous closure/hemostasis time; healthcare facility overcrowding a concern. Need for a vascular closure approach enabling ambulatory PVI. DESIGN/SETTING: Prospective single-arm observational study (named PRO-PVI); included a 1:1 propensity-matched control cohort (usual workflow) for time-to-discharge comparison. POPULATION/N: 50 patients enrolled, scheduled for PVI. INTERVENTION: Perclose Proglide suture-mediated vascular closure device for femoral venous access closure after PVI, in an ambulatory/same-day-discharge workflow. COMPARISON: propensity-matched control cohort using standard/usual workflow (for discharge timing). ENDPOINTS: feasibility = % discharged same day of procedure; efficacy = acute access-site closure rate, time to hemostasis, time to ambulate, time to discharge; safety = vascular complications at 30 days; plus patient satisfaction and cost (direct + indirect). RESULTS (exact numbers): same-day discharge in 96% of patients; device deployment success 100%; immediate hemostasis (<1 min) in 30/50 patients = 62.5%; mean time to discharge 5:48 +/- 1:03 h vs 10:16 +/- 1:21 h in matched cohort, P < 0.0001; no major vascular complication occurred; high patient satisfaction with post-operative time; cost analysis showed neutral impact vs standard of care. TAKEAWAY: Suture-mediated femoral venous closure after PVI enables safe same-day discharge within ~6 h in 96% of patients, halving post-op recovery/discharge time, with no major vascular complications, improved patient satisfaction, and cost-neutral impact; could reduce healthcare facility overcrowding.
